evals.prompt: I'm implementing PredictionEngine for Cadence. What unit tests do I need to write? Give me the complete test list with the exact edge cases the skill requires.
evals.expected_output: A complete list of all 10 required PredictionEngine edge cases from §3, including the zero-cycles default, 0-1/2-3/4+ confidence thresholds, SD=2.0 boundary, open period exclusion, max-6-cycles cap, and algorithm spot-check. Must mention fixed anchor dates instead of Date().
expectations: Lists all 10 required edge cases from §3
Mentions SD=2.0 boundary case as explicitly required
Warns against using Date() — requires fixed anchor date
Mentions open period (endDate nil) exclusion test

evals.prompt: We're about to ship the first TestFlight build of Cadence. What's our coverage gate? Which layers need 80%+ and how do we verify it?
evals.expected_output: States the 80%+ gate on data and domain layers as a release gate (not nice-to-have). Lists PredictionEngine at 90%+, privacy filter at 90%+, SwiftData models at 80%+, ViewModels at 75%+. Explains how to verify with xccov and stresses per-file inspection, not just aggregate.
expectations: Explicitly calls out 80%+ as a release gate, not a metric
Warns that 80% aggregate masking 0% on PredictionEngine is a gate failure
Provides xccov command or equivalent verification method

evals.prompt: Writing UI tests for Cadence's Log Sheet. How should I set up the test class, isolate from live data, find elements, and what exact flows must I cover?
evals.expected_output: Specifies --in-memory-store and --uitesting launch arguments, continueAfterFailure = false, accessibilityIdentifier-based element lookup (not display text), and covers all required flows: sheet presents from center tab, period started, chip toggle, notes input, isPrivate toggle, save dismisses.
expectations: Requires accessibilityIdentifier for element lookup, not display text
States that no live Supabase connection is permitted
